name: Faun
description: Variable-character filter with continuous slopes across four response types. Adds timbre ripple, dual inputs, V/oct and FM control, and enough gain to move from clean sweeps into saturated grit.

controls.name: Slope
controls.description: Sets the curve of the filter across the four response types.

controls.name: Timbre
controls.description: Adds ripple inside the passband and lifts the internal gain as it rises.

controls.name: Input Gain
controls.description: Sets the drive on the variable-gain input path.

controls.name: V/Oct
controls.description: Tracks cutoff for pitched sweeps and resonant lines.

controls.name: FM
controls.description: Adds FM to the cutoff with attenuation and polarity control.

inputs.name: Audio In 1
inputs.description: Unity-gain input optimized for signals up to +-5V.

inputs.name: Audio In 2
inputs.description: Variable-gain input with attenuator for feedback patching or extra drive.

inputs.name: V/Oct CV
inputs.description: Pitch tracking input for cutoff sweeps and oscillator-style use.

behaviors.name: Continuous slope morph
behaviors.description: A single control moves the filter smoothly across the four response types instead of switching hard between modes.

behaviors.name: Passband ripple
behaviors.description: Timbre introduces comb-like ripple in the passband and changes the tone from smooth to vocal or liquid.

behaviors.name: Gain staging
behaviors.description: The variable-gain input allows feedback and drive, letting the module shift from clean filter into saturated coloration.

behaviors.name: Pitch tracking
behaviors.description: The filter can be played like a pitched resonant source when cutoff tracks 1V/oct.

behaviors.name: Cutoff FM
behaviors.description: Frequency modulation adds motion without changing the base slope setting.

patchIdeas.name: Clean sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Feed a saw into the unity input · keep Timbre low · sweep Slope slowly from lowpass to notch · the module stays precise while the curve changes under your hand.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Faun:
* Slope: 9 o'clock
* Timbre: low
* Input Gain: unity

VCO:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> Faun (Audio In 1)
- Faun (Lowpass) -> Output (L)
- Faun (Highpass) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Liquid formant
patchIdeas.patch: Use Timbre to add ripple and a slow LFO into FM · the bandpass and notch outputs move between vocal and liquid formant regions while the pitch stays centered.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Faun:
* Slope: noon
* Timbre: 2 o'clock
* Input Gain: unity

LFO:
VCO:
Output:

- VCO (Out) -> Faun (Audio In 1)
- LFO (Out) >> Faun (FM CV)
- Faun (Bandpass) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Saturated acid line
patchIdeas.patch: Drive the variable input hard and raise Timbre until the filter starts to bite · the output becomes a crisp, acid-leaning line with grit instead of polite resonance.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Faun:
* Slope: 1 o'clock
* Timbre: high
* Input Gain: max

VCO:
Envelope:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> Faun (Audio In 2)
- Envelope (Out) >> Faun (FM CV)
- Faun (Lowpass) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Pitched notch drone
patchIdeas.patch: Patch V/Oct from a sequencer and listen to the notch output while modulating FM lightly · the result sits between filter and oscillator, especially with a high Timbre setting.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Faun:
* Slope: highpass
* Timbre: 1 o'clock

Sequencer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Faun (V/Oct CV)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> VCA (CV)
- Faun (Notch) -> Output (In)
